name: Timed Up & Go
description: Standard Timed Up & Go (TUG) test. Participant places phone in front trouser pocket. The app detects stand-up and the first 3 m of walking (with stride/cadence metrics), plays an audio cue at 3 m, then waits for the sit-down impact spike to stop the timer (back-dated to the impact moment). Turn and walk-back phases are not measured electronically — only the overall TUG time and the clean 3 m walk-out gait segment are recorded.

fields.description: Unique identifier for this session. Used for deduplication on re-sync.
UTC timestamp of when the test began (when the first 'start' timer event fired).
Total duration of the sensor recording session, from first to last event.
Clinical TUG time: stand-up to sit-down impact, in seconds. Primary performance metric. Lower = faster. Clinical thresholds: <10s normal, 10-20s variable, >=13.5s fall-risk threshold.
Duration of the stand-up phase (rising from chair) detected by accelerometer impact + sustained tilt.
Step count during the first 3 m of walking (walk-out segment).
Estimated distance during the walk-out segment (Weinberg stride). Should be close to 3.0 m.
Duration of the walk-out segment, from stand-up complete to 3 m beep.
Mean stride length over the walk-out segment (Weinberg estimator from vertical acceleration peaks/valleys).
Coefficient of variation of stride length (SD / mean). Higher values indicate more irregular gait.
Cadence over the walk-out segment: steps * 60 / duration_seconds.
Mean inter-step interval over the walk-out segment.
Coefficient of variation of inter-step interval. Validated predictor of gait variability and fall risk.
Gait speed over the walk-out segment: distance / duration_seconds. Clinical cutoff <0.8 m/s suggests mobility impairment.
Self-reported fatigue level at the time of assessment. Null if not answered.
Whether the participant had taken their medication on the day of assessment. May be null if skipped.
Walking aid used during the TUG test. Selected by participant in setup screen.
Whether this session was flagged as potentially unreliable on the results screen.

fields.example: Phone fell out of pocket mid-test
fields.description: Free-text reason provided when session is flagged. Null if not flagged.
Full raw sensor and timer event stream. Motion: { kind, t, ax, ay, az, gx, gy, gz }. Timer: { kind, t, event, source }.
SHA-256 checksum of the raw_data array for integrity verification.
Operating system and version of the device.
Screen (viewport) width at time of assessment.
Screen (viewport) height at time of assessment.
Version of the MAC Spine app that recorded the session.
